name: tailwind-css-expert
description: Comprehensive Tailwind CSS utility-first CSS framework expert covering Tailwind v4.1, utility classes, responsive design, dark mode, CSS variables, P3 colors, grid layout, filters, transitions, cascade layers, logical properties, container queries, gradients, 3D transforms, and production optimization

can_do: Explain Tailwind CSS utility-first approach and philosophy
Provide Tailwind CSS class names and utilities
Show responsive design patterns with breakpoint prefixes
Explain dark mode implementation with dark: prefix
Demonstrate CSS variables theme customization with @theme
Show P3 wide gamut color usage
Provide filter and backdrop filter utilities
Explain cascade layers (@layer theme, base, components, utilities)
Demonstrate logical properties for RTL/LTR support
Explain automatic CSS purging and production optimization
Recommend Tailwind Plus components and templates
cannot_do: Write custom CSS (focuses on utility classes)
Design complete layouts (provides utilities, not designs)
Override utility classes directly (uses cascade layers instead)

what_is_tailwind.statement: Tailwind CSS is a utility-first CSS framework that uses classes directly in HTML markup instead of separate CSS files. Classes like flex, pt-4, text-center, rotate-90 can be composed to build any design. Version 4.1 is current.

philosophy.statement: Utility-first approach: build designs by composing small, single-purpose utility classes rather than writing custom CSS. Enables rapid development without leaving HTML.
philosophy.example: Use flex flex-col items-center instead of writing .container { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; }

breakpoints.statement: Tailwind uses mobile-first responsive design with breakpoint prefixes: sm: (small), md: (medium), lg: (large), xl: (extra large). Add breakpoint prefix before any utility class to apply at that screen size. Base classes apply to mobile by default.
breakpoints.example: class="flex flex-col md:flex-row lg:grid lg:grid-cols-3" applies flex-column on mobile, flex-row on medium, grid with 3 columns on large screens

mobile_first.statement: Default utilities apply to mobile screens. Add sm:, md:, lg:, xl: prefixes for larger breakpoints. This is mobile-first responsive design pattern.
mobile_first.example: class="text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg" - small text on mobile, base on medium, large on large screens

dark_mode_implementation.statement: Add dark: prefix to any color class to apply it in dark mode. Tailwind automatically handles dark mode detection based on user's system preferences or manual toggle.
dark_mode_implementation.example: class="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white" - white background on light mode, gray-800 on dark mode

css_variables.statement: Customize Tailwind theme using CSS variables in @theme block. Define custom fonts, colors, spacing, typography scales using CSS custom properties. Uses oklch() color space for wide gamut colors.

p3_colors.statement: Tailwind v4.1 uses P3 wide gamut color palette automatically, providing more vibrant colors without requiring manual configuration. Full color range: red, orange, amber, yellow, lime, green, emerald, teal, cyan, sky, blue, indigo, violet, purple, fuchsia, pink, rose. Each color has shades 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950.

flexbox.statement: Flexbox utilities: flex (display: flex), flex-col (flex-direction: column), flex-row (flex-direction: row), items-center (align-items: center), justify-between (justify-content: space-between), gap-{size} (gap spacing).

grid.statement: CSS Grid utilities: grid (display: grid), grid-cols-{n} (grid-template-columns: repeat(n, minmax(0, 1fr))), grid-rows-{n}, gap-{size} (gap), auto-cols-{size}. Grid can be used directly in HTML markup.

container_queries.statement: Container queries allow children to adapt to container size, not viewport. Use @container class on parent, then @sm:, @md:, @lg: prefixes on children for container-based responsive design.

padding_margin.statement: Spacing utilities: p-{size} (padding all sides), px-{size} (padding horizontal), py-{size} (padding vertical), pt/pb/pl/pr-{size} (padding single side). Same pattern for margin: m-, mx-, my-, mt-, mb-, ml-, mr-. Size scale: 0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64.

text_utilities.statement: Typography utilities: text-{size} (font-size), font-{weight} (font-weight: normal, medium, semibold, bold), text-{align} (text-align: left, center, right, justify), leading-{size} (line-height), tracking-{size} (letter-spacing).

background_text.statement: Color utilities: bg-{color}-{shade} (background-color), text-{color}-{shade} (color), border-{color}-{shade} (border-color). Full palette with wide gamut P3 colors: red, orange, amber, yellow, lime, green, emerald, teal, cyan, sky, blue, indigo, violet, purple, fuchsia, pink, rose. Shades: 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950.

filters.statement: Filter utilities: blur-{size} (blur-sm, blur-md, blur-lg), brightness-{value}, grayscale, contrast-{value}, saturate-{value}, sepia. Can be stacked for multiple effects.

gradients.statement: Gradient utilities: bg-gradient-to-{direction} (to-r, to-l, to-t, to-b, to-tr, to-tl, to-br, to-bl), from-{color}-{shade}, via-{color}-{shade}, to-{color}-{shade}.

shadows.statement: Shadow utilities: shadow-{size} (shadow-sm, shadow, shadow-md, shadow-lg, shadow-xl, shadow-2xl), shadow-{color}-{shade} for colored shadows.

2d_transforms.statement: Transform utilities: scale-{value} (scale-50, scale-100, scale-125), rotate-{deg} (rotate-45, rotate-90, rotate-180), translate-x-{size}, translate-y-{size}, skew-x-{deg}, skew-y-{deg}.

3d_transforms.statement: 3D transform utilities: perspective-{size}, preserve-3d, transform-style: preserve-3d for 3D space transformations. Can scale, rotate, and translate elements in 3D.

transition_utilities.statement: Transition utilities: transition (transition property), duration-{ms} (duration-150, duration-300, duration-500, duration-750), ease-{type} (ease-linear, ease-out, ease-in-out, ease-in), delay-{ms}. Can be combined.

cascade_layers.statement: Tailwind uses CSS cascade layers (@layer) to avoid specificity issues. Layers defined: theme (CSS variables), base (Preflight styles), components (custom components), utilities (utility classes). Order: @layer theme, base, components, utilities;

logical_properties.statement: Tailwind supports logical properties for RTL/LTR text direction. Classes automatically adapt based on text direction. Utilities use logical properties internally (start/end instead of left/right).
logical_properties.example: class="ps-4 pe-4" (padding-start, padding-end) works for both LTR and RTL

setup.statement: Install Tailwind CSS: npm install -D tailwindcss. Then in CSS file: @import "tailwindcss";. Use utilities directly in HTML/JSX className prop. Tailwind scans files for class usage and generates only used CSS.

optimization.statement: Tailwind automatically removes all unused CSS when building for production. Final CSS bundle is minimal - most Tailwind projects ship less than 10kB of CSS to the client. This is automatic purging based on class usage scanning.
optimization.example: Build process scans HTML/JSX files, finds all utility classes used, generates only those CSS rules

react_nextjs.statement: Tailwind works seamlessly with React (className prop) and Next.js (automatic optimization). Can be used alongside component libraries like PrimeReact. TypeScript support for type-safe class names.
react_nextjs.example: Next.js: automatically optimizes Tailwind CSS, works with className prop in JSX

tailwind_plus.statement: Tailwind Plus provides ready-made components: Templates (React/Next.js site templates), UI Blocks (500+ responsive components), UI Kit (starter kit for component systems). Additional resources: Headless UI (accessible components), Heroicons (SVG icons).
tailwind_plus.example: Use Tailwind Plus for pre-built components to accelerate development
